rulings.Case Title: RICHARD CRESSE VS. RINA ACCOUNTANTS & ADVISORS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Unopposed Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement And Memorandum Of Points And Authrorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, May-14-2026. Line 1. 2-PLAINTIFF RICHARD CRESSE's, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHER SIMILARLY SITUATED, Unopposed Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement. The unopposed motion is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. The court will sign the proposed final approval order lodged with the moving papers. The parties must submit a separate final judgment, which should indicate the two class members who have opted out. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff Steve Lakovits' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two (Nos. 22, 36, 48, 93, 94, 96, 99, 101, 102, And 139), To Defendant Brick And Timber, Inc. And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, May-14-2026. Line 2. 1-PLAINTIFF STEVE LAKOVITS', AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two (Nos. 22, 36, 48, 93, 94, 96, 99, 101, 102, And 139), To Defendant Brick And Timber, Inc. And Request For Monetary Sanctions. The unopposed motion to compel further responses to plaintiff Steve Lakovits' second set of requests for production of documents, numbers 22, 36, 48, 93, 94, 96, 99, 101, 102, and 139, is granted. Defendant Brick and Timber Inc.'s objections lack merit. Defendant shall serve further responses within two weeks and make full production within 30 days. Lakovits seeks sanctions. The court concludes Brick and Timber's objections lack substantial justification but awards partial sanctions because the motion was unopposed. Brick and Timber shall pay $1920 to Lakovits within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MELLETT ET AL VS. CRAIG S. MILLER ESQ. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Gregory S. Walston, Esq. To Provide Further Responses To Form Interrogatory Numbers 2.6(B) And 4.1; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Gregory S. Walston, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, May-14-2026. Line 4. 1-PLAINTIFFS ROBERT MELLETT, AN INDIVIDUAL, and MULCAHY, LLC's, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, Motion To Compel Defendant Gregory S. Walston, Esq. To Provide Further Responses To Form Interrogatory Numbers 2.6(B) And 4.1; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Gregory S. Walston, Esq. Plaintiffs Robert Mellett et al. ("Mellett")'s motion to compel further responses to form interrogatory numbers 2.6(b) and 4.1 is denied. With respect to 2.6(b), defendant Gregory S. Walston's March 2026 response provided a complete response. The court was not inclined to order a further response, but Walston has mooted the motion by making a further response regardless. With respect to 4.1, although Walston interposed objections, his response is complete, and his opposition confirms that no responsive information is withheld. Mellett posits there is further information that Walston should have included, such as coverage through the Walston law office, but his showing falls short of proving there is information Walston has withheld. Generally a motion to compel further responses is not a proper vehicle to get a party to change the content of its responses. Mellett may propound additional targeted discovery to learn whether his assertion that the Walston firm has separate insurance that may cover the incident is correct. Both parties seek sanctions. The court concludes that Mellett's motion lacks substantial justification and orders Mellett to pay partial sanctions of $1000 to defendants within 30 days. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER JOHNSON ET AL VS. PAUL HEUBERGER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs' Motion For Leave To Conduct Financial Discovery On Defendants Paul Heuberger, And Suzanne Nguyen Heuberger
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, May-14-2026. Line 6. PLAINTIFFS CHRISTOPHER JOHNSON, and MELANIE JOHNSON's Motion For Leave To Conduct Financial Discovery On Defendants Paul Heuberger, And Suzanne Nguyen Heuberger. Plaintiffs' motion for leave to conduct financial condition discovery is denied. Discovery into a defendant's financial condition is subject to Civil Code, Sec. 3295, which prohibits such discovery absent court order. "[B]efore a court may enter an order permitting discovery of a defendant's financial condition, it must (1) weigh the evidence submitted in favor of and in opposition to motion for discovery, and (2) make a finding that it is very likely the plaintiff will prevail on his claim for punitive damages." (Jabro v. Superior Court (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 754, 758.) "Very likely" means more than a reasonable probability, and more than a prima facie showing of the kind needed to defeat summary judgment. (Id.) The court concludes, based on a review of all of the evidence presented by the parties on this motion, that plaintiffs have not met their burden of showing it is very likely they will prevail on a claim for punitive damages. In particular, defendants present evidence that they relied on the advice of counsel in their dealings with plaintiffs. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
